title: Analyze
use_when: Answer data questions from quick lookups to full analyses. Use when looking up one metric, investigating a trend or drop, comparing segments over time, or preparing a formal data report for stakeholders.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Analyze when the request is mainly a different lifecycle artifact; route to the narrower Productize skill that owns that artifact instead. Do not use it when the user lacks enough context to distinguish facts, assumptions, and decision risk.
output_artifact: Analyze UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
framework_fit: Analyze fits Analytics work in the Design lifecycle when the user needs Analyze UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks and has enough product context to make tradeoffs explicit. Use it to produce a decision-ready artifact, not a framework tour.
failure_modes: Produces Analyze UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks without tying recommendations to the user's Design stage, evidence, and decision pressure.
Treats Analyze as generic Analytics advice instead of naming assumptions, missing inputs, risks, and the next validation step.
Fails to route away from Analyze when another Productize skill owns the requested artifact more directly.

examples.prompt: Use $analyze to turn this data context into a decision-ready Analyze UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks.
examples.expected_artifact: Analyze UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
